code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 4 Academic Writing

functional_suitability.note: Description is a single sentence without trigger contexts; scope is narrower than the skill actually delivers

reliability.note: Clarification-first rule well implemented; gap: no explicit handling when figures/tables are described textually but not provided

agent_usability.note: Seven reference modules are well organized but the 'component-alignment-rules.md' may over-check sections not provided, requiring more granular handling

human_usability.note: Sample triggers are natural and user-facing; good discoverability

agent_specific.note: Good escape hatches (clarification-first, insufficient input warning); trigger precision slightly low due to brief description

inputs.label: Full manuscript pre-submission consistency check: title/abstract/methods/results/figures alignment

assertions.text: Review scope determined (full-manuscript vs targeted) before analysis
Sample-size inconsistencies across sections checked
Figure/table numbering alignment verified
Conclusion-result mismatch explicitly checked
Severity classification applied to all findings

inputs.label: Revision-stage version drift check after multiple reviewer rounds

assertions.text: Endpoint wording drift between original and revised submission identified
Version-specific changes tracked and their consistency implications noted
True inconsistency distinguished from acceptable section-specific summarization
Correction priority plan provided for major drift items
No fabricated manuscript content added to fill gaps

inputs.label: Supplement reference consistency and figure legend alignment check

assertions.text: Supplement citations in main text matched against supplement table/figure list
Figure legend content checked against results section prose
Numbering mismatches flagged as their own issue category
Harmless ordering preferences not flagged as errors
Missing supplement content handled with 'uncertain — material not available' label

inputs.label: Only abstract and discussion provided — no methods or results sections
inputs.note: Clarification-first rule triggered; partial review produced

assertions.text: Clarification-first rule applied, missing sections explicitly identified
Partial review limited to available sections only
Skill does not guess at missing methods or results content
Recommended upload items specified (Methods, Results, full draft)
Partial review findings appropriately labeled as 'limited by available material'
assertions.note: Two observations in the partial review section were stated as inconsistencies without sufficient qualification that they might resolve once full manuscript is available

inputs.label: Large multi-cohort manuscript with complex endpoint hierarchy and supplemental appendix
inputs.note: Some minor items missed in complex multi-cohort structure

assertions.text: Primary endpoint vs secondary endpoint hierarchy consistent across all sections
Sample size reported consistently across all cohort descriptions
assertions.note: Complex multi-cohort structure caused one sub-cohort sample count discrepancy to be missed in the stress test

assertions.text: Supplement appendix citations all verified against main text
Figure numbering and panel labels checked across all cited locations
assertions.note: Panel-level label consistency (e.g., Figure 3A vs Figure 3a) was not checked at panel letter level

assertions.text: Correction priority plan addresses all major risks

key_strengths: Clarification-first rule prevents overconfident consistency claims when manuscript sections are missing
Seven reference modules covering all major consistency check types (version drift, figure/table linkage, conclusion boundaries) provide thorough coverage
Severity classification mandatory — prevents flat stylistic review that treats all issues as equally important
Natural, user-facing sample triggers provide strong discoverability for manuscript authors

recommendations.title: Panel-level figure label consistency not checked in complex manuscripts
recommendations.problem: In large manuscripts with many multi-panel figures, the skill checked figure-level numbering but missed panel-level label consistency (e.g., Figure 3A vs Figure 3a, or panel label drift between legend and callout in results text).
recommendations.root_cause: figure-table-supplement-linkage-rules.md likely covers figure-level checks but panel-level sub-item checking is not explicitly mandated.
recommendations.fix: Add panel-level label checking to the figure-table-supplement-linkage-rules.md reference: explicitly verify that panel letters in figure legends match those called out in results text, and that uppercase/lowercase conventions are consistent.

recommendations.title: Partial-context findings not consistently labeled as provisional
recommendations.problem: When only partial manuscript sections are available, some inconsistency observations are stated without qualifying that they may resolve once the full manuscript is reviewed.
recommendations.root_cause: The clarification-first rule instructs limiting the review to available material but does not explicitly require provisional labeling of all findings made under incomplete context.
recommendations.fix: Update the clarification-first reference: any finding made in a partial review must include a one-sentence qualifier '(provisional: may resolve with full manuscript access)'.

recommendations.title: Description is too brief to differentiate from generic proofreaders
recommendations.problem: A one-sentence description about 'internal contradictions and version drift' does not communicate the breadth of check types (endpoint drift, figure numbering, supplement linkage, conclusion-result mismatch) to users unfamiliar with the skill.
recommendations.root_cause: Description not optimized for triggering coverage across the full range of user scenarios.
recommendations.fix: Expand description to include key trigger phrases: 'endpoint drift', 'figure-table mismatch', 'version drift after revision', 'sample size inconsistency', 'conclusion-result mismatch'.
